1. BODY SURFACE AREA (BSA) — Rule of Nines
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Body Region              Patient's Palm = ~1% BSA      % Affected
Head & Neck (9%):        _______% of region affected → _______%  of total BSA
Upper Extremities (18%): _______% of region affected → _______%  of total BSA
Trunk (36%):             _______% of region affected → _______%  of total BSA
Lower Extremities (36%): _______% of region affected → _______%  of total BSA
Genitalia (1%):          _______% of region affected → _______%  of total BSA

                                          TOTAL BSA AFFECTED: _______%
                         Protocol Threshold: ≥ 10% at Screening and Baseline

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. PASI SCORING — By Body Region
   Score each parameter 0-4:
   0 = None, 1 = Slight, 2 = Moderate, 3 = Severe, 4 = Very Severe
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

                        HEAD      UPPER       TRUNK     LOWER
                        (h)      EXTREMITIES  (t)      EXTREMITIES
                                   (u)                    (l)
Erythema (E):          _____      _____       _____      _____
Induration (I):        _____      _____       _____      _____
Desquamation (D):      _____      _____       _____      _____
Sum (E+I+D):           _____      _____       _____      _____

Area Score (A):        _____      _____       _____      _____
  (0 = 0%, 1 = 1-9%, 2 = 10-29%, 3 = 30-49%, 4 = 50-69%, 5 = 70-89%, 6 = 90-100%)

Body Region Weight:     0.1        0.2         0.3        0.4

Region PASI = (E+I+D) × A × Weight:
   Head:               _____ × _____ × 0.1 = _______
   Upper Extremities:  _____ × _____ × 0.2 = _______
   Trunk:              _____ × _____ × 0.3 = _______
   Lower Extremities:  _____ × _____ × 0.4 = _______

                                         TOTAL PASI SCORE: _______
                              Protocol Threshold: ≥ 12 at Screening and Baseline
                              Primary Endpoint: PASI 75 (≥75% reduction from baseline)

Baseline PASI (if post-baseline visit): _______
% Change from Baseline: _______%
PASI 50 Achieved?  [ ] Yes  [ ] No
PASI 75 Achieved?  [ ] Yes  [ ] No
PASI 90 Achieved?  [ ] Yes  [ ] No

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. INVESTIGATOR'S GLOBAL ASSESSMENT (IGA) — 5-Point Scale
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Score    Definition                                              Circle One
  0      Clear: No signs of psoriasis. Post-inflammatory           [ 0 ]
         hyperpigmentation may be present.
  1      Almost Clear: No thickening. Normal to pink                [ 1 ]
         coloration. No to minimal focal scaling.
  2      Mild: Just detectable to slight thickening.                [ 2 ]
         Pink to light red coloration. Fine scaling
         over < 50% of the lesion.
  3      Moderate: Clearly distinguishable thickening.              [ 3 ]
         Dull to bright red/dark red. Moderate scaling.
  4      Severe: Severe thickening with hard edges.                 [ 4 ]
         Bright to dark red. Severe/coarse scaling
         covering ≥ 50% of the lesion.

                                        IGA SCORE: _______
                          Protocol Threshold: ≥ 3 at Screening and Baseline
                          Secondary Endpoint: IGA 0 or 1 with ≥ 2-grade improvement

Baseline IGA (if post-baseline visit): _______
IGA Response (0 or 1 with ≥ 2-grade improvement)?  [ ] Yes  [ ] No  [ ] N/A

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. PRURITUS NUMERIC RATING SCALE (NRS)
   "On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you rate your itch at its worst
    in the past 24 hours?"
   0 = No itch at all          10 = Worst itch imaginable
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

                                 Pruritus NRS Score: ___ / 10

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
